Why are you interested in this role?: This role is basically the two things I love most made into one job: keeping a complex process running like clockwork, and meeting new people. At InGenius Prep I own end-to-end scheduling and logistics for 20+ programs across Marketing, Tech, and Sales teams—and I genuinely enjoy being the person who makes sure everyone shows up informed, prepared, and on time. I'm also a 97% extrovert who loves putting people at ease. Cursor being small, fast, and building the team from the ground up means a coordinator actually shapes what the company becomes, and that's exactly the kind of impact I want.
What makes you a great fit / what are yo: People describe me as incredibly reliable and the most organized person they've worked with—I'm the one who triple-checks the timezone before sending the invite. I stay calm while holding many moving pieces together, and I see inefficiency as a personal challenge: at InGenius I'm constantly finding small improvements to how we operate, and I built documentation for 70+ staff that cut onboarding time by 75%. I pair that operational rigor with genuine warmth—I care about the candidate who flew across the country, the hiring manager trying to make the right call, and the interviewer running between meetings, and I want it to be great for all of them.
Experience with Ashby / ATS?: I'll be honest—I haven't used Ashby specifically, but I have extensive Greenhouse administration experience: scheduling interviews, emailing candidates, posting roles, and keeping the pipeline and data clean. Keeping an ATS accurate so nothing slips is exactly the kind of detail work I obsess over, and I pick up new tools quickly, so I'm confident Ashby would click for me fast.
Why Cursor?: I'm drawn to Cursor because it's small, talent-dense, and truth-seeking—the people you hire now literally shape what the company becomes, which makes a coordinator role feel high-leverage rather than back-office. I also love that you build fast and care about craft. My training in Applied Organizational Psychology makes me treat process as a system to improve, and I'd be excited to bring both that rigor and real warmth to every candidate who comes through your Soho office.
